skill.name: copy-editing
skill.description: Edit, review, and improve existing marketing copy through seven focused sweeps for clarity, tone, benefits, proof, specificity, emotion, and action.
skill.summary: Improves marketing copy with a structured multi-pass editing framework.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: The eight static weak cryptography alerts are false positives from prose in SKILL.md, not cryptographic code. No scripts, network calls, filesystem operations, external commands, or prompt injection attempts were found in the reviewed Markdown skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Crypto Match in Description
low_findings.description: The static alert at line 3 matches ordinary frontmatter description text. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher configuration, or executable code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML metadata describing copy editing use cases. The weak crypto signal is from prose, not code.

low_findings.title: Duplicate False Positive Weak Crypto Match in Description
low_findings.description: A second static alert points to the same description line. This is duplicate prose evidence and does not indicate cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The duplicate line 3 finding has the same non-code context. There is no implementation surface for weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Crypto Match in Benefit Guidance
low_findings.description: The static alert at line 106 appears in a sentence about reader desires. It is copy-editing guidance, not a security-sensitive algorithm choice.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is natural language inside the So What editing sweep. It contains no code, imports, commands, or data handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Crypto Match in Specificity Heading
low_findings.description: The static alert at line 157 appears in a heading for specificity upgrades. It does not reference or configure a cryptographic primitive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a Markdown heading in an editing framework. The alert is a substring match inside prose.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Crypto Match in Emotion Guidance
low_findings.description: The static alert at line 197 appears in the phrase about desire for transformation. This is marketing copy guidance, not cryptographic usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 197 is a bullet under emotional dimensions. There is no executable or configuration content.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Crypto Match in Editing Checklist
low_findings.description: The static alert at line 308 appears in checklist text about identifying a desired action. It is safe instructional prose.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line belongs to a pre-editing checklist. It has no code path, command, network action, or secret handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Crypto Match in Benefit Checklist
low_findings.description: The static alert at line 326 appears in checklist text about connecting benefits to desires. It is not a cipher or hashing instruction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is copy review guidance. No cryptographic terms are used as algorithms or implementation choices.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Crypto Match in Copy Problem Fix
low_findings.description: The static alert at line 373 appears in a fix recommending a desired outcome. It is prose and presents no security risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is part of a common copy problems section. It does not execute or instruct use of weak cryptography.

content.user_title: Improve Marketing Copy with Focused Editing
content.value_statement: Marketing copy often loses impact because edits try to fix everything at once. This skill applies focused sweeps for clarity, tone, proof, benefits, specificity, emotion, and action.
actual_capabilities: Runs a seven-sweep review covering clarity, tone, benefits, proof, specificity, emotion, and action.
Identifies confusing wording, weak claims, missing proof, vague language, and unclear calls to action.
Suggests specific edits while preserving the original message and author voice.
Provides quick word, sentence, and paragraph checks for shorter reviews.
Guides iterative collaboration by checking earlier edits after each pass.
limitations: Works best on existing copy, not blank-page copywriting.
Needs audience, goal, brand voice, and available proof for stronger recommendations.
Cannot verify external claims unless evidence is provided.
Does not guarantee conversion lift without testing.

use_cases.title: Review a Landing Page
use_cases.description: Find unclear claims, weak proof, vague benefits, and risky calls to action before publishing.

use_cases.title: Polish Founder-Written Copy
use_cases.description: Keep the founder voice while making product messaging clearer, more specific, and easier to act on.

use_cases.title: Standardize Client Feedback
use_cases.description: Use repeatable editing sweeps to give structured copy feedback across web, email, and ad projects.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Copy Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a short draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this marketing copy with the copy-editing skill. Focus on clarity, voice, and obvious improvements first: [paste copy].

prompt_templates.title: Seven-Sweep Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the seven copy-editing sweeps on this draft. Show issues, explain why they matter, and suggest revised wording: [paste copy].

prompt_templates.title: Conversion Page Polish
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed review for a page with a call to action.
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this landing page for conversion. Check benefits, proof, specificity, emotion, objections, and CTA clarity: [paste copy].

prompt_templates.title: Iterative Brand Voice Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this copy in stages. Preserve this brand voice: [describe voice]. Audience: [audience]. Goal: [goal]. Copy: [paste copy].

output_examples.input: A SaaS homepage headline and subhead that feel vague.
output_examples.output: The review flags abstract wording, missing reader benefit, and weak proof. It suggests a clearer headline tied to a measurable outcome.

output_examples.input: An email call to action with objections left unanswered.
output_examples.output: The edit identifies friction near the CTA, adds trust signals, clarifies the next step, and softens unsupported claims.

output_examples.input: A product description with mixed formal and casual tone.
output_examples.output: The feedback marks tone shifts, recommends consistent wording, and checks that clarity survives the voice edits.

best_practices: Provide the target audience, goal, and desired action before asking for edits.
Ask for one sweep at a time when the draft is long or high stakes.
Supply proof, customer quotes, and product facts so claims can be strengthened safely.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to invent proof, statistics, testimonials, or customer results.
Do not use it as the only review for legal, medical, or regulated claims.
Do not request a full rewrite when you need feedback on preserving voice.

faq.question: What type of copy is this skill best for?
faq.answer: It is best for existing marketing copy, including landing pages, emails, ads, product pages, and calls to action.

faq.question: Can it write new copy from scratch?
faq.answer: It is designed for editing existing copy. For new drafts, provide a starting version before using this skill.

faq.question: Does it preserve brand voice?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow asks for voice and tone consistency while preserving the original message.

faq.question: Can it verify factual claims?
faq.answer: It can flag unsupported claims, but it needs provided evidence to verify or strengthen them.

faq.question: How detailed is the review?
faq.answer: You can request a quick pass or the full seven-sweep framework for a deeper review.

faq.question: Is this useful for conversion optimization?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks benefits, proof, specificity, emotional relevance, objections, and calls to action.
